message:
chore(beads): sync REVIEW duplicate-bead reconciliation metadata for 9sztj + wtp2t

Routine beads metadata sync on two duplicate REVIEW entries filed in
the earlier fresh-eyes sweep:

- `flywheel_connectors-9sztj — [REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/spotify:
  handshake test masks connector handshake semantics`
- `flywheel_connectors-wtp2t — [REVIEW][MED] fcp-e2e/webhook_receiver:
  happy-path suite never proves endpoint listing behavior`

Both were filed alongside near-duplicate siblings (9ftlo/a6ouw for
spotify, u2g49 for webhook) in the prior 8-bead commit. This sync
flips a status/metadata field on each (line count unchanged: 2
modifications, 0 insertions, 0 deletions) as part of the normal
dedup-and-link workflow the reviewers use when consolidating
parallel findings. No new issues filed; no existing issue bodies
changed.

files.patch:
@@ -1047,7 +1047,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","title":"[FCP3.VERIFY] Runtime invariants and deterministic failure-forensics harness","description":"## Goal
Define the deterministic runtime invariant and failure-forensics program for the FCP3 execution model.

## Why this exists
When concurrency and lifecycle semantics get subtle, simple pass or fail outcomes are not enough. We need a harness that catches invariant violations and preserves enough evidence to explain them later.

## Scope
- quiescence, loser-drain, cancellation safety, supervision restart behavior, and obligation-leak detection
- durable state and receipt correctness under cancellation, restart, or partial failure
- evidence bundles suitable for deep debugging rather than shallow pass or fail only
- deterministic fault injection and schedule-shaping where useful for reproducing edge cases

## Deliverables
- invariant catalog for the runtime and host lifecycle model
- failure-forensics harness expectations and artifact rules
- reusable assertions that pilot and conformance scenarios can inherit

## Acceptance criteria
A future engineer can reproduce and diagnose lifecycle or concurrency failures from retained evidence instead of depending on luck, timing, or rereading the runtime code.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:43.320949Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:14:12.330953Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:14:12.330480Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","forensics","runtime","verify"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:07.986382Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:26:13.729686Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:26:13.791601Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:08.121729Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:43.320949Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1469,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.1","author":"RainyGate","text":"[Alien-graveyard uplift]

High-EV fit from the graveyard for this bead is **§3.10 Hindsight Logging** plus **§6.20 Deterministic Simulation Testing** and the Deterministic Debugging recipe. The harness should not just inject faults; it should record the minimal nondeterministic inputs needed to replay concurrency failures, then support bounded schedule exploration on top of that evidence.

Concrete implications for this bead:
- artifact bundles should preserve the minimum replay substrate: scheduling choices, timer order, message delivery order, lease/receipt transitions, and external-fault injections
- deterministic harness mode should be treated as a first-class runtime/testing surface, not a thin test helper
- failure forensics should support shrinking/minimizing traces to the smallest reproducer, not just storing giant logs
- safe mode should cap exploration depth/time and fall back to captured trace replay only when search budgets are exhausted

This sharpens the difference between ordinary logging and actually replayable concurrency evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:55:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","title":"[FCP3.VERIFY] Connector archetype conformance, performance budgets, and long-run soak evidence","description":"## Goal
Define the connector archetype conformance and long-run evidence program for FCP3.

## Why this exists
FCP3 only deserves confidence if each connector style is proven against the semantics that matter in practice: state correctness, cancellation, backpressure, restart, failover, resource discipline, and operator-visible evidence.

## Scope
- archetype-specific conformance matrices for request-response, streaming, and stateful/background connectors
- expectations for unit, integration, and end-to-end evidence attached to each archetype class
- long-run soak scenarios for host plus connectors under restart, fault, and load variation
- performance and resource budgets with retained artifacts, baselines, and regression criteria
- explicit logging and replay expectations so failures remain diagnosable after the fact

## Deliverables
- conformance matrix templates reusable by future connector families
- soak and performance scenario catalog with artifact expectations
- acceptance rules that tie migration completion to evidence, not intuition

## Acceptance criteria
A future connector migration wave cannot be declared done unless it satisfies the archetype matrix, produces replayable evidence, and stays within declared resource and performance budgets.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:43.466084Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:15:11.248218Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:15:11.247917Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fcp3","perf","soak","verify"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:49.859626Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:54.936387Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.14.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.072243Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:08.529386Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:53.699032Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:08.393422Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:08.664013Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:43.466084Z","created_by":"jemanuel","meta  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","title":"[FCP3.VERIFY] Adversarial, hostile-service, and security scenario corpus with replayable evidence","description":"## Goal
Define the adversarial and security scenario corpus for FCP3 across hostile peers, malformed frames, credential failures, policy denials, replay attempts, and split-brain or fence-violation risks.

## Why this exists
FCP is explicitly security-first. Unit tests and fuzzing are necessary but not sufficient; we also need scenario-level proof that the host, SDK, placement model, and operator surfaces behave correctly when the environment is actively unhelpful or hostile.

## Scope
- hostile or malformed host-connector peer interactions beyond parser-level fuzzing
- credential expiry, revocation, rotation, and misbinding scenarios
- denied capability, denied placement, denied egress, and denied approval flows with operator-facing evidence
- replay, duplicate delivery, stale checkpoint, and fence-token conflict scenarios
- recovery expectations and retained evidence bundles for postmortem diagnosis

## Deliverables
- reusable adversarial scenario catalog across local, WASI, and remote/mesh contexts
- explicit assertions for what must fail closed versus what may degrade gracefully
- replayable end-to-end scenarios with detailed structured logging and redaction-safe evidence bundles

## Acceptance criteria
A future engineer cannot declare FCP3 operationally trustworthy without satisfying this corpus for the relevant connector archetypes and execution environments.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:19:40.995356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:14:54.502873Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:14:54.502559Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","security","test","verify"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:20:00.574118Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:20:01.228047Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:20:00.558037Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:20:00.581951Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:19:40.995356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-9sztj","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/spotify: handshake test masks connector handshake semantics","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/spotify_compliance_e2e.rs:89-120 serializes the real HandshakeRequest but discards the connector response () and fabricates a HandshakeResponse with fresh session_id, echoed nonce, and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/spotify/src/connector.rs:223-249 only returns protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities. This suite will stay green if the connector handshake stops surfacing required session semantics because the adapter invents them. Fix: assert directly on the connector handshake payload or make the connector emit a real HandshakeResponse-compatible shape and validate that end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:53.315371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:53.315371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-9sztj","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-e2e/spotify: handshake test masks connector handshake semantics","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/spotify_compliance_e2e.rs:89-120 serializes the real HandshakeRequest but discards the connector response () and fabricates a HandshakeResponse with fresh session_id, echoed nonce, and echoed requested capabilities. connectors/spotify/src/connector.rs:223-249 only returns protocol_version/connector_id/version/capabilities. This suite will stay green if the connector handshake stops surfacing required session semantics because the adapter invents them. Fix: assert directly on the connector handshake payload or make the connector emit a real HandshakeResponse-compatible shape and validate that end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:53.315371Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:37.826684Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:37.826412Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-a6ouw (same spotify handshake finding)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9ut","title":"fcp.arxiv: Citation graph traversal (cited-by and references)","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Citation graph traversal (cited-by and references) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide citation graph access to support literature review workflows:
- references (outgoing)
- cited-by (incoming)
- small bounded subgraph traversal

## Data source constraints
arXiv’s API does **not** provide complete citation data. This bead supports two strategies:
1. **Preferred**: query an external citation index (e.g., Semantic Scholar) when policy enables it.
2. **Fallback**: parse references from PDF/LaTeX (best-effort, lower accuracy).

## Scope
- Get references for a paper.
- Get cited-by for a paper (preferred via external index).
- Build bounded citation subgraph:
  - max depth
  - max nodes

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.citations.read`
- `semanticscholar.citations.read` (optional dependency)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to whichever source is enabled.
- Output is bounded and deterministic.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.citations.references`
  - `arxiv.citations.cited_by`
  - `arxiv.citations.subgraph`
- Canonical output type:
  - `CitationGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - graph bounds enforcement
  - deterministic traversal ordering
  - error mapping when citation source unavailable

## Acceptance criteria
- Citation graph traversal is useful, bounded, and policy-controlled.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:14:33.678529733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T19:23:29.283402Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T19:23:29.283385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9ut","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9ut","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8yxg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":785,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9ut","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Covered by main arXiv connector build (8yxg). All operations implemented with tests.","created_at":"2026-03-05T19:23:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9xi8x","title":"[MOCK] zalo planned operations scaffold shipped","description":"connectors/zalo/src/connector.rs:12, 103-110, 130-177 - the connector reports successful configure/handshake, advertises bot/message/update/webhook operations in introspection, but marks every operation implemented=false and hard-refuses invoke/simulate with a scaffold-only 'planned but not implemented' response. Real behavior should implement the declared identity/message/update/webhook operations on the live path, or stop advertising them as runtime surface until execution exists.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:54:17.541599Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:20.840745Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:20.840082Z","close_reason":"Runtime guard already present and enforced — connectors/zalo/src/connector.rs:130 returns FcpError::InvalidRequest{code:1002, 'planned but not implemented in this connector slice'} on invoke and handle_simulate returns {allowed:false, simulate_capability:unsupported, reason:'... not implemented yet'}. Introspect explicitly sets implemented=false on every operation + surface_status='incubating'. Existing test at line 265 asserts the unimplemented guard. Full runtime implementation of the declared bot/message/update/webhook operations is deferred to the per-connector roadmap bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Scene activation and management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support scene discovery and activation for common automation workflows.

## Scope
- List scenes.
- Activate a scene.
- Optional: create/update/delete scenes (dangerous; defer unless needed).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.scenes.read`
- `ha.scenes.activate` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `ha.scenes.write` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Scene activation is a side effect:
  - receipts + audit events include scene id + action

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `ha.scene.list`
  - `ha.scene.activate`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - activate flow
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Scenes can be activated safely and auditable.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:16:46.454681406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:22:23.115382Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:22:23.115323Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ortf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ortf.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1118,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9y2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Scenes implemented: homeassistant.list_scenes + homeassistant.activate_scene. Tests passing. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:22:22Z"}]}
@@ -2241,7 +2241,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wke.5","title":"fcp.qdrant: Provisioning Automation (Endpoint + Auth)","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeOwl","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:14:45.087684634Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-02T07:55:06.994243Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T07:55:06.994230Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-wke.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-wke","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-wke.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-wke.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","title":"[MOR/C2.2] Version the operational model: V1 host-first (current) vs V2 mesh-native (target)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:11.543957Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:58:39.101008Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T23:58:39.100294Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","documentation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:28.015154Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2635,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Create an explicit operational model versioning scheme. V1 = Host-First (current, proven, operational). V2 = Mesh-Native (target, designed, not yet proven). Document what each version provides, what it does not, and what the transition path looks like. Add timeline or explicit no-committed-timeline statement. WHY: I4 Perspective found transition is unversioned and roadmap is implicit. E1 Belief-Revision found the gap creates false confidence. F3 Counterfactual noted mesh is CORRECT long-term choice but incomplete. Without explicit versioning, operators and agents cannot reason about which features are available NOW vs PLANNED. WHERE: New section in README.md or new file docs/OPERATIONAL_MODEL_VERSIONS.md. Also update docs/FWC_Host_First_Truthfulness_Playbook.md to reference versions. ACCEPTANCE: (1) V1 (Host-First) clearly defines what operators can rely on today: fwc -> fcp-host, host-backed answers, single-active-host deployment. (2) V2 (Mesh-Native) clearly defines what it will provide: mesh-backed answers, automatic failover, multi-node placement, offline operation. (3) Explicit statement: either timeline for V2 or no committed timeline yet. (4) Each fwc command documented with which version it requires. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2 days.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:16Z"},{"id":2666,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.2 complete. Created docs/OPERATIONAL_MODEL_VERSIONS.md with V1/V2 definitions, per-command version table, transition milestones, explicit no-committed-timeline. Added Operational Model Versions section to README. Updated playbook to reference versions. Commit 7ae2248a.","created_at":"2026-04-10T23:58:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wnee","title":"FIX: fcp-core clippy warnings (doc_markdown, map_unwrap_or)","description":"## Clippy Warnings Fixed

Fixed 3 clippy warnings in `crates/fcp-core/src/credential.rs`:

1. **doc_markdown** (line 232): Added backticks around `::1` in IPv6 example
2. **map_unwrap_or** (lines 238-240): Replaced `.map().unwrap_or()` with `.map_or()`
3. **doc_markdown** (line 258): Added backticks around `host_allow`

## Files Changed

- `crates/fcp-core/src/credential.rs`

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wwq8","title":"[FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness","description":"# [FCP2] DeviceProfile: Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Execution Fitness

## Goal
Define and implement the DeviceProfile schema used by the execution planner to make device-aware routing and execution decisions.

## Why This Matters
The spec envisions operations routing to devices based on capabilities (GPU, TPU, memory, battery, network). Without a concrete DeviceProfile schema, execution planning is guesswork.

## DeviceProfile Schema (NORMATIVE)

### Hardware Capabilities
```
DeviceProfile {
  node_id: TailscaleNodeId,
  profile_version: u32,
  timestamp: u64,
  
  // Compute
  cpu_cores: u16,
  cpu_arch: CpuArch,  // x86_64, aarch64, wasm32
  memory_mb: u32,
  gpu: Option<GpuProfile>,
  tpu: Option<TpuProfile>,
  
  // Storage
  local_storage_mb: u64,
  symbol_store_quota_mb: u32,
  
  // Power
  power_source: PowerSource,  // Mains, Battery(percent), Solar
  battery_percent: Option<u8>,
  
  // Network
  bandwidth_estimate_kbps: u32,
  latency_class: LatencyClass,  // Local, LAN, Internet, DERP
  metered: bool,
  
  // Availability
  availability: AvailabilityProfile,  // AlwaysOn, Scheduled, BestEffort
  next_expected_downtime: Option<u64>,
  
  // Installed Connectors
  connectors: Vec<InstalledConnector>,
}
```

### GpuProfile
```
GpuProfile {
  vendor: GpuVendor,  // Nvidia, AMD, Apple, Intel
  model: String,
  vram_mb: u32,
  compute_capability: Option<String>,  // CUDA version, etc.
}
```

### InstalledConnector
```
InstalledConnector {
  connector_id: ConnectorId,
  version: Version,
  binary_hash: ObjectId,
  capabilities: Vec<CapabilityGrant>,
}
```

## Profile Publication
- Nodes MUST publish DeviceProfile to mesh on:
  - startup
  - significant capability change
  - connector install/remove
  - periodic refresh (every 5min default)
- Profile is a canonical mesh object (content-addressed)
- Profiles are gossipped to all zone members

## Execution Fitness Scoring
The execution planner uses DeviceProfile to compute fitness:
```
fitness = base_score
  - derp_penalty (if would use DERP)
  + locality_bonus (if symbols already present)
  - battery_penalty (if on battery < 20%)
  + capability_bonus (if has required GPU/TPU)
  - latency_penalty (based on latency_class)
```

## Tests
- Unit tests: DeviceProfile serialization roundtrip
- Unit tests: fitness scoring with various profiles
- Integration tests: profile gossip propagation
- Property tests: fitness ordering is stable and deterministic

## Acceptance Criteria
- DeviceProfile schema is canonical and versioned
- Execution planner can query available profiles and compute fitness
- Profile changes trigger re-evaluation of pending operations

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-wyz0","title":"TEST: Tailscale Integration Unit Tests (MeshIdentity, NodeKeyAttestation, Peer Discovery)","description":"# TEST: Tailscale Integration Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate Tailscale integration (identity, attestations, peer discovery, ACL mapping) with deterministic tests/mocks.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for Tailscale mesh identity and peer discovery.

## Test Categories

### MeshIdentity
- [ ] Node ID derived from WireGuard pubkey
- [ ] Identity is stable across restarts
- [ ] Identity is unique per device
- [ ] Identity format is canonical

### NodeKeyAttestation
- [ ] Attestation binds node signing key to mesh identity
- [ ] Attestation is owner-signed
- [ ] Attestation includes timestamp
- [ ] Stale attestation detection
- [ ] Attestation verification

### Peer Discovery
- [ ] Peers discovered via Tailscale API
- [ ] Peer list updates on join/leave
- [ ] Peer health monitoring
- [ ] Peer capability detection
- [ ] Peer filtering by ACL tags

### ACL Tag Mapping
- [ ] tag:fcp-owner maps to z:owner
- [ ] tag:fcp-private maps to z:private
- [ ] tag:fcp-work maps to z:work
- [ ] tag:fcp-community maps to z:community
- [ ] tag:fcp-public maps to z:public
- [ ] Unknown tags handled gracefully

### Transport Security
- [ ] FCPS uses WireGuard encryption
- [ ] Additional AEAD layer verified
- [ ] No plaintext fallback
- [ ] Connection failures logged

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/tailscale/`
- `mesh_identity.cbor`
- `node_key_attestation.cbor`
- `peer_discovery_response.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_peer_discovery",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "node_id": "...",
  "peers_discovered": 3,
  "acl_tags": ["tag:fcp-work"],
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mesh identity is stable and unique
- Peer discovery works reliably
- ACL tags map correctly to zones
